subject: Computer Networking
title: Site Surveys and Heat Maps
description: Learn Site Surveys and Heat Maps in one minute.

hook.text: Before installing APs, engineers walk the building with a laptop. They're making a Wi-Fi heat map.

buildup.text: A site survey measures signal strength, noise, and interference. Software turns data into a color map.

discovery.text: Red zones have strong signal. Blue zones are weak. The map shows exactly where to place APs.

twist.text: Walls, metal, water, and people absorb Wi-Fi. A pretty floor plan can be a terrible radio environment.

climax.text: Predictive surveys simulate AP placement before install. Optimize coverage without drilling a hole.

punchline.text: A heat map turns Wi-Fi guesswork into precise engineering.

quiz.question: What does a Wi-Fi site survey measure?
options: Signal strength, noise, and interference at each location
The number of devices connected to each AP
The internet speed from the ISP
